findings.summary: logging_utils.py accumulates 119 importers and owns formatting, progress, timing, decorators, emoji constants, and environment detection — too many unrelated responsibilities for one module
evidence: logging_utils.py re-exports JSONFormatter, TemplateFormatter, Spinner, StreamingProgress, calculate_eta, calculate_eta_ema, calculate_eta_with_confidence, log_progress_bar, log_progress_streaming, log_with_spinner from split modules, then adds its own ProjectLogger, log_operation, log_timing, log_function_call, log_success, log_header, log_progress, log_stage, log_substep, log_resource_usage, set_global_log_level, EMOJIS dict, USE_EMOJIS flag, USE_STRUCTURED_LOGGING flag, and pytest-environment detection logic all in one file

The split into logging_formatters/logging_helpers/logging_progress is incomplete: logging_utils still holds the union of all public API in __all__ (700+ lines), so callers import from the hub rather than the focused submodule — no boundary gain was achieved
findings.suggestion: Give each submodule a stable public path (e.g. callers import log_success from infrastructure.core.logging_success, not from logging_utils). Reduce logging_utils to a thin re-export shim or remove it in favour of direct submodule imports.

findings.summary: infrastructure/publishing/core.py contains no logic of its own — it is entirely noqa:F401 re-exports from citations.py, metadata.py, models.py, and platforms.py, plus one unrelated imported helper from file_operations
evidence: Lines 24-47 of core.py are entirely `from ... import ... # noqa: F401` statements re-exporting symbols from four sibling modules

The sole non-import line in core.py is `from infrastructure.core.file_operations import calculate_file_hash` followed by `create_publication_package` which uses it — this is the only actual logic in the file
publishing/__init__.py then re-exports from core.py, adding a third indirection layer (caller -> __init__ -> core -> citations/metadata/platforms)
findings.suggestion: Collapse core.py into __init__.py so the package surface is explicit in one place, and delete the intermediate hub file. Alternatively, keep core.py only for logic that truly belongs there (create_publication_package) and import the citation/metadata symbols directly in __init__.py from their owning modules.

findings.summary: query(), query_short(), query_long(), and query_structured() each independently repeat the same start_time/generation_time/logger.debug("completed") timing pattern rather than sharing a single measurement boundary
evidence: query() at lines 180, 218: `start_time = time_module.time()` then `generation_time = time_module.time() - start_time` with logger.debug('Query completed', extra={...generation_time_seconds...})
query_long() at lines 426, 453: identical pattern duplicated verbatim with logger.debug('Long query completed', extra={...})
query_structured() at lines 500, 545: same pattern a third time with logger.debug('Structured response received', ...)
query_short() also duplicates the same structure (lines ~370-406)
findings.suggestion: Extract a single `_timed_generate(prompt, model, options, mode_label)` internal method that handles timing and debug logging once; the four public methods set up their specific options and delegate to it.

findings.summary: The three-line pytest environment detection block is copy-pasted verbatim into both setup_logger() and get_logger(), creating dual maintenance points for the same runtime probe
evidence: setup_logger() lines 257-261: `is_test_env = (os.getenv('PYTEST_CURRENT_TEST') is not None or 'pytest' in sys.modules or any('pytest' in str(v) for v in sys.modules.values() if hasattr(v, '__file__')))`
get_logger() lines 327-331: identical three-condition expression repeated word-for-word
findings.suggestion: Extract to a module-level `_is_test_environment() -> bool` function and call it from both sites.

findings.summary: log_header() computes a value and immediately discards it with no effect
evidence: Line 515: `EMOJIS['rocket'] if USE_EMOJIS else ''` — the expression is evaluated but the result is never assigned to a variable or used anywhere in the function body
findings.suggestion: Either assign the result to a variable and use it as a prefix in the log calls, or remove the dead expression entirely.

findings.summary: An exception while reading any single markdown file sets all cross-reference categories to False and breaks the loop — single-file failure contaminates the entire result set
evidence: Lines 125-128: `except Exception as e: logger.error(...); integrity = {k: False for k in integrity}; break` — a read error on one file resets all five integrity keys (equations, figures, tables, sections, citations) to False and stops processing remaining files
findings.suggestion: Record the failing file as an individual error entry and continue processing remaining files; only mark integrity False for the categories where actual missing references were detected.

findings.summary: query_long() manually unpacks options.temperature, options.seed, options.stop field-by-field into a new GenerationOptions rather than merging/inheriting, making callers' options partially silently dropped
evidence: Lines 440-445: `long_options = GenerationOptions(max_tokens=self.config.long_max_tokens, temperature=options.temperature if options else None, seed=options.seed if options else None, stop=options.stop if options else None)` — other GenerationOptions fields (top_p, top_k, repeat_penalty, num_ctx) passed by the caller are silently discarded
findings.suggestion: Use dataclasses.replace(options, max_tokens=self.config.long_max_tokens) if options else GenerationOptions(max_tokens=...) so callers' options are merged rather than partially dropped.

findings.summary: clean_output_directories() mixes directory enumeration, log archiving, selective file preservation, empty-dir pruning, and subdir creation — five distinct responsibilities in one function body
evidence: The function body (lines 96-220) contains: project-name discovery via filesystem scan, call to clean_root_output_directory, log file archiving with timestamped copies, preserved-path logic for corpus/nanopublications files, checkpoint directory skipping, selective _clean_dir_preserving dispatch, and finally recreating the standard subdirectory tree — all sequentially in a single function
findings.suggestion: Extract archive_logs(), preserve_incremental_files(), and recreate_output_structure() as separate, named helpers so clean_output_directories() reads as a composition of well-named steps.
